item.title: Enhancing Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) competency in gastroenterology fellowship through traditional, digital, and experiential learning pathways.

item.title: Translation and cross-cultural adaptation of the Identification of Spondyloarthritis Questionnaire (IBIS-Q) into Danish for patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - Spondyloarthritis (SpA) affects 10-30% of patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). An early diagnosis is essential as a delayed diagnosis of SpA increases the risk of disability and reduces ...

item.title: Evaluation of the quality, reliability of Crohn's disease-related content on Douyin and Xiaohongshu with insights from user comments: A cross-sectional study.
item.description: Digital health - No studies have evaluated Crohn's disease-related video quality on short video platforms. This study assesses the quality, reliability, and audience engagement of such videos on Douyin and Xiaohong...

item.title: Fibrin glue with mesenchymal stem cells for perianal ﬁstulas: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

item.title: Frailty Is Associated With All-Cause and Acute Hospitalisations During 18 Months Follow-Up in Older Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Guidelines recommend considering frailty in the management of older patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD). A geriatric assessment is a specific and clinically applicable frailty assessment...

item.title: A mixed methods study of multidisciplinary team assessment and therapeutic decision making for intestinal strictures in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Scientific reports - Crohn's disease (CD) is frequently complicated by intestinal strictures, which substantially affect patients quality of life and long-term outcomes. Accurate classification of strictures-as inflamm...

item.title: Can Mycobacterium avium subsp. paratuberculosis Survive During Pecorino Cheesemaking Process? An Experimental Evaluation Through a Microbiological Challenge Test.
item.description: Journal of food science - Paratuberculosis is an infectious disease mainly affecting domestic ruminants, caused by Mycobacterium avium subsp. paratuberculosis (MAP). Affected animals shed the pathogen through feces, also du...

item.title: Effectiveness and safety of dual advanced therapy in inflammatory bowel disease: a multicenter series from Galicia, Spain.

item.title: GLIM criteria: from consensus to practice in inflammatory bowel disease.
Potential of pharmacogenetics in treatment of chronic inflammatory diseases - a danish report overview from 2000 - 2024.
item.description: The pharmacogenomics journal - Review - Given the substantial degree of inter-individual variability in treatment responses in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), treatment optimization is warranted. We provide an overview of...

item.title: Definitions, diagnosis, management, and outcomes of upper gastrointestinal Crohn's disease: an international, expert RAND/UCLA appropriateness study.
item.description: The lancet. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Patients with Crohn's disease can have isolated or co-existent upper gastrointestinal involvement, but this is an understudied clinical manifestation. There are neither standardised definitions nor...

item.title: Correction: ACG Clinical Guideline Ulcerative Colitis in Adults.

item.title: Efficacy of Mediterranean Diet for the primary prevention of autoimmune diseases: A systematic review and meta-analysis featured in the Italian National Guidelines "La Dieta Mediterranea".
item.description: Nutrition (Burbank, Los Angeles County, Calif.) - Meta-analysis - The Mediterranean Diet (MD) has been associated with reduced inflammation and improved immune regulation, suggesting a potential protective role against autoimmune diseases. This review was conduct...

item.title: Factors leading to implant failure: An umbrella review of meta-analyses of observational studies and trials.
item.description: The Journal of prosthetic dentistry - Systematic review - The evidence concerning factors leading to implant failure remains inconclusive. Existing systematic reviews have reported mixed results for patient-related, surgical, and prosthetic factors contri...

item.title: Interventions for improving treatment adherence in young people with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD): A systematic review of behaviour change theory and behaviour change techniques.
item.description: Journal of child health care : for professionals working with children in the hospital and community - Systematic review - Treatment adherence is important but challenging for young people with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Behavioural interventions may support adherence, leading to improved condition management. T...
